_comment: Candidate = same model, stricter grounding prompt. Fixes the q6 gap (now names both cartridges), slightly longer answers (a little more cost), similar latency. Expected gate verdict: PASS.

q1.text: Open Settings > Print > Duplex and toggle it on. Duplex printing is supported on the PrintFlow PF-300 and later models. [doc:d1]

q2.text: The default print resolution is 300 dpi. You can raise it to 1200 dpi under Settings > Print > Quality [doc:d1]; note that higher resolutions increase ink usage. [doc:d2]

q3.text: Supported paper sizes are A4, Letter and Legal; the rear tray also accepts envelopes. [doc:d1] The supported paper weight range is 60-220 gsm. [doc:d2]

q4.text: Press and hold the WPS button for 3 seconds, or use the PrintFlow app under Device > Network. [doc:d1] The status LED blinks blue while pairing. [doc:d2]

q5.text: Open the rear tray, pull the sheet out slowly in the paper path direction, then close the tray; the E13 error clears automatically. [doc:d1] Never pull the paper against the feed direction. [doc:d2]

q6.text: The PrintFlow PF-500 uses the INK-52 black cartridge and the INK-53 tri-color cartridge. [doc:d1]

q7.text: Install firmware updates from the PrintFlow app under Device > About > Firmware > Update; the printer must stay powered on during the update. [doc:d1]

q8.text: Yes. Use the PrintFlow app on iOS or Android, or print via standard AirPrint. [doc:d1] The phone and printer must share a network. [doc:d2]
